properties.remark: MOST ROADS IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK ARE CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING FROM PROLONGED MODERATE TO HEAVY RAIN. EVACUATIONS ARE TAKING PLACE IN LOW LYING AREAS WITH DEBRIS AND WATER UP TO 8 INCHES DEEP FLOWING ACROSS ROADS.
HIGHWAY 62 WAS CLOSED FROM STATE ROUTE 177 TO VIDAL JUNCTION DUE TO FLOODING AND DEBIS IN THE ROADWAY.
MEASURED AT THE RED ROCK MESOWEST SITE LOCATED AT AN ELEVATION OF 3756 FEET.
MEASURED AT NTS STATION 28 WHICH IS LOCATED AT AN ELEVATION OF 3631 FEET.
MEASURED AT THE REBOUND ANGLE ROAD EAST NTS SITE WHICH IS LOCATED AT AN ELEVATION OF 3994 FEET.
A WIND GUST OF 58 MPH WAS MEASURED AT THE REBOUND MEDIA ROAD MESONET SITE AT AN ELEVATION OF 3990 FEET.
A 59 MPH WIND GUST WAS MEASURED AT THE REBOUND ANGLE ROAD MESONET SITE AT AN ELEVATION OF 3994 FEET.
A WIND GUST OF 60 MPH WAS RECORDED AT THE AREA 3 NORTH MESONET SITE AT AN ELEVATION OF 4130 FEET. A 58 MPH WIND GUST WAS RECORDED AT 515 PM.
A 62 MPH WIND GUST WAS MEASURED AT THE AREA 16 MESONET SITE AT AN ELEVATION OF 4558 FEET.
A 62 MPH WIND GUST WAS RECORDED AT THE DESERT ROCK ASOS ALONG WITH VISIBILITY DROPPING TO ONE MILE IN BLOWING DUST.
SEVEN POWER POLES WERE KNOCKED DOWN BY STRONG WINDS ON THE SOUTHWEST PART OF THE NEVADA TEST SITE. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
SEVERAL BOATS WERE DAMAGED AT THE LAS VEGAS BOAT HARBOR ON LAKE MEAD. BOAT DOCKS WERE ALSO DAMAGED.
A 59 MPH WIND GUST WAS RECORDED AT THE NORTH LAS VEGAS AIRPORT.
A 63 MPH WIND GUST WAS MEASURED AT THE NELLIS AIR FORCE BASE ASOS SITE WHICH IS LOCATED AT AN ELEVATION OF 1870 FEET. SUSTAINED WINDS OF 51 MPH WERE ALSO RECORDED.
A LARGE TREE WAS UPROOTED AND FELL ONTO A HOUSE CAUSING DAMAGE TO THE ROOF OF IT. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
A SPOTTER IN CALIENTE REPORTED THAT A MICROBURST KNOCKED DOWN A DOZEN TREES AT THE CALIENTE YOUTH CENTER.
THE ELLA MT. LOOKOUT REPORTED SUSTAINED WINDS AROUND 40 MPH WITH GUSTS UP TO 61 MPH.

THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED THAT SEVERAL PEOPLE WERE STRANDED IN VEHICLES IN THE CASTLE MOUNTAIN AREA OF THE MOJAVE NATIONAL PRESERVE ABOUT 10 MILES UP HART MINE ROAD DUE TO FLOODWATERS. THE FLOODWATERS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE UP TO 2 FEET DEEP AND WERE FLOWING SLOWLY.
A FEW ROCKS WERE WASHED ACROSS ROADS IN THE DOLAN SPRINGS AREA. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
STORM SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER-SIZED HAIL IN BOULDER CITY.
STORM SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER-SIZED HAIL IN KINGMAN ARIZONA.
TRAINED SPOTTER MEASURED QUARTER SIZED HAIL.
A PARK RANGER REPORTED 8 INCHES OF WATER ON MEADVIEW BOULEVARD AND THAT ROADS WERE BEING BLOCKED OFF DUE TO FLOODING.
HEAVY RAIN AND WATER OVER THE ROADS AFTER 1.14 INCHES OF RAIN FELL BETWEEN 1000AM AND 1100AM.
HEAVY RAIN RESULTED IN WATER OVER ROADS AFTER 1.14 INCHES OF RAIN FELL BETWEEN 1000AM AND 1100AM.
THE U.S. FOREST SERVICE REPORTED THAT ROCKS AND DEBRIS WERE WASHED ONTO ROADS ON MOUNT CHARLESTON. A SNOW PLOW WAS DISPATCHED TO CLEAN THE ROCKS IN ONE INSTANCE.
THE FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED MODERATE FLOODING ON ROADS NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF PIERCE FERRY ROAD AND STOCKTON HILL ROAD.
A SPOTTER REPORTED SEVERAL HOMES WITH ROOF TILES BLOWN BACKWARDS AND 2 PORCHES WERE BLOWN OVER BETWEEN BULLHEAD CITY AND MOHAVE VALLEY.
DIAMOND BAR ROAD WAS CLOSED FROM PIERCE FERRY ROAD EAST TO THE HUALAPAI INDIAN RESEVATION BOUNDARY.
WATER AND DEBRIS FLOWED ACROSS HIGHWAY 62 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 64 AND 67. CALTRANS HAD TO PLOW AWAY THE DEBRIS.
CHP WEBSITE REPORTED THAT NHP REQUIRED CALTRANS TO CLOSE STATE ROUTE 164 NEAR NIIPTON FOR FLOODING IN THE AREA.
A 58 MPH WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH OUTFLOW FROM A THUNDERSTORM WAS RECORDED AT THE HENDERSON EXECUTIVE AIRPORT. VISIBILITY WAS ALSO REDUCED TO 5 MILES IN BLOWING DUST.

THREE 15 FOOT TALL MESQUITE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AT THE CORNER OF PECOS ROAD AND PEBBLE ROAD IN HENDERSON. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED. WIND GUSTS IN THE AREA WERE ESTIMATED AT 40 TO 45 MPH.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED 2.19 INCHES OF RAIN FELL TO CAUSE MINOR FLOODING WITH ROCK AND MUD IMPACTING THE ROADWAY.
THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST FROM THE NORTHWEST OF 64 MPH MEASURED AT THE KINGMAN AIRPORT.
THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST FROM THE NORTHWEST OF 64 MPH MEASURED AT THE KINGMAN AIRPORT.
THE VALLE VISTA COUNTRY CLUB REPORTED WATER WITH ROCKS...BOULDERS...AND OTHER DEBRIS ACROSS SEVERAL ROADWAYS IN THE VALLE VISTA AREA.
SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED AND BLOWN OVER BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS ALONG BULLHEAD CITY PARKWAY. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
THE OWNER OF THE NIPTON TRADING POST REPORTED FLOWING WATER FROM A NEARBY WASH ACROSS NIPTON ROAD WITH DEBRIS.
SEVERAL MOBILE HOME TIPPED OVER AND BLOWN ACROSS THE ROADWAY. WINDS WERE MEASURED IN GOLDEN VALLEY AT 61 MPH.
61 MPH GUST CAUSED DAMAGED TO PATIO FURNITURE AND LIGHT FIXTURES.
A 61 MPH GUST CAUSED DAMAGED TO PATIO FURNITURE AND LIGHT FIXTURES.

*** 8 INJ *** EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED THAT 20 RV/MOBILE HOMES DESTROYED AT HIGHWAY 95 AND AZTEC ROAD IN FORT MOHAVE. EIGHT PEOPLE WERE INJURED WITH TWO SERIOUS. TWO HOMES RECEIVED WATER/MUD DAMAGE. DAMAGE WAS INITIALLY ESTIMATED AT 500,000 DOLLARS.
*** 6 INJ *** 12 MOBILE HOME TRAILERS WERE BLOWN OVER. 5 PEOPLE RECEIVED MINOR INJURIES WITH ONE ELDERLY PERSON SERIOUSLY INJURED.
AMATEUR RADIO OPERATOR IN GOLDEN VALLEY REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 67 MPH.
AN AMATEUR RADIO OPERATOR IN GOLDEN VALLEY REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL. THE HAIL BROKE ALL THE WEST WINDOWS OF THE HOUSE...BROKE HIS WEATHER STATION...AND GOT ALL HIS RADIO EQUIPMENT WET.
*** 4 INJ *** 7 MOBILE HOME TRAILERS WERE BLOWN OVER.3 PEOPLE RECEIVED MINOR INJURIES WITH ONE ELDERLY PERSON SERIOUSLY INJURED.
THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE REPORTED EXTENSIVE FLOODING DUE TO HEAVY RAINFALL HAD OCCURRED ON THE EASTERN PART OF CEDAR CANYON ROAD NEAR LANFAIR ROAD.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL FELL ON THE MOJAVE NATIONAL PRESERVE NEAR CEDAR CANYON ROAD AND LANFAIR ROAD.

ALL MAJOR WASHES WERE FULL ALONG IVANPAH ROAD IN THE EASTERN PART OF THE MOJAVE NATIONAL PRESERVE. RUNNING WATER FROM THE WASHES WASHED OUT ASPHALT ON IVANPAH ROAD SOUTH OF NEW YORK MOUNTAIN. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.

US HIGHWAY 95 WAS CLOSED BETWEEN THE INTERCHANGE WITH INTERSTATE 40 AND MILE MARKER 60 DUE TO FLOODING IN MULTIPLE SPOTS AND ROCK AND DIRT ON THE ROADWAY. AT LEAST ONE VEHICLE WAS STUCK ON THE ROAD DUE TO FLOODING.
THE KINGMAN SHERRIFFS OFFICE REPORTED OVER ONE FOOT OF WATER RUNNING ACROSS THE INTERSECTION OF PIERCE FERRY RD AND CHARLES DRIVE IN SOUTH MEADVIEW.
A SOUTHEAST WIND GUST OF 78 MPH WAS MEASURED AT THE US-93 AND KAISER BRIDGE MESONET SITE.
